tickerText: [TENNIS] Bad Homburg Open week: Naomi Osaka beats Magdalena Frech 6-4, 6-1 after a rain delay; Qinwen Zheng grinds past Solana Sierra 7-5, 4-6, 6-4; Clara Tauson snaps a seven-match skid with a 6-4, 6-4 upset of defending champion Diana Shnaider; Elise Mertens halts Alexandra Eala's momentum 6-3, 6-3 • [SQUASH] PSA Squash Tour Finals wrap in Paris: Diego Elias becomes the first South American season champion, rallying past Paul Coll 7-11, 11-4, 11-6, 11-9 for $108,000; world No. 1 Hania El Hammamy claims her second Tour Finals crown with an 11-6, 10-11, 11-8, 11-8 win over Sivasangari Subramaniam as the 2025/26 campaign closes • [BADMINTON] Macau Open crowns champions at the East Asian Games Dome: Hu Zhe An rallies from a game down to beat Kantaphon Wangcharoen 11-21, 21-10, 21-13 for his first Super 300 title; Kim Ga Eun tops Park Ga Eun 21-16, 21-13; Jiang Zhen Bang and Wei Ya Xin reunite for mixed doubles gold • [PICKLEBALL] PPA Asia 500 Beijing Open closes at China's National Tennis Center: Jack Wong Hong-kit defeats Zane Ford 11-5, 11-8; Sahra Dennehy wins women's singles and doubles but falls short of a triple crown in mixed; Portland PPA Challenger opens June 26 in Maine • [TABLE TENNIS] WTT Star Contender Ljubljana at Hala Tivoli: Shunsuke Togami beats Hugo Calderano for men's singles and pairs with Hiroto Shinozuka for doubles; Hina Hayata survives a seven-game final against Zhu Yuling and doubles with Miwa Harimoto; Korea's Lim Jong-hoon and Shin Yu-bin take mixed gold ahead of the $1.55M United States Smash starting June 26

newsItems.title: Osaka and Zheng Advance as Shnaider Falls in Bad Homburg
newsItems.excerpt: The Bad Homburg Open delivered early Wimbledon signals on Monday. Naomi Osaka completed a rain-delayed 6-4, 6-1 win over Magdalena Frech and will face Elise Mertens, who stopped Berlin semifinalist Alexandra Eala 6-3, 6-3. Qinwen Zheng rallied past Solana Sierra 7-5, 4-6, 6-4 in her grass return, while Clara Tauson upset defending champion Diana Shnaider 6-4, 6-4 to snap a seven-match losing streak. Anna Kalinskaya and Xinyu Wang also reached round two.

newsItems.title: Elias and El Hammamy Crown PSA Tour Finals in Paris
newsItems.excerpt: The 2025/26 PSA Squash Tour season ended at Centquatre-Paris with two champions sharing $108,000 each. Peru's Diego Elias became the first South American to win the season finale, coming from a game down to beat Paul Coll 7-11, 11-4, 11-6, 11-9 in 82 minutes for his 23rd tour title. World No. 1 Hania El Hammamy claimed her second PSA Tour Finals crown and seventh title this season with an 11-6, 10-11, 11-8, 11-8 victory over Malaysia's Sivasangari Subramaniam.

newsItems.title: Hu Zhe An Breaks Through at Macau Open
newsItems.excerpt: China's Hu Zhe An captured his first HSBC BWF World Tour title at the SANDS CHINA LTD. Macau Open, rallying from a game down to defeat Thailand's Kantaphon Wangcharoen 11-21, 21-10, 21-13. The 2024 world junior champion denied Wangcharoen a first title in over nine years. South Korea's Kim Ga Eun beat compatriot Park Ga Eun 21-16, 21-13 in women's singles, while China swept mixed and women's doubles and Korea's Jin Yong and Lee Jong Min took men's doubles.

newsItems.title: Wong and Dennehy Dominate Beijing Open at Olympic Venue
newsItems.excerpt: PPA Tour Asia's Capital Securities Beijing Open concluded at China's National Tennis Center with Jack Wong Hong-kit defeating top seed Zane Ford 11-5, 11-8 for men's singles gold. Australia's Sahra Dennehy overwhelmed Chao Yi Wang 11-7, 11-3 in the women's final and later paired with Yufei Long for doubles gold, but Wang and Len Yang denied her a triple crown with an 11-7, 11-6 mixed-doubles win in the Olympic Green venue's pickleball debut.
